Policies › Remote Work and BYOD Policy

Remote Work and BYOD Policy

Remote Work and BYOD Policy

Document Version: 2.3
Effective Date: 15 March 2023
Last Review: 22 September 2024
Next Review: March 2025
Document Owner: Jordan Reeves, People & Culture Coordinator
Approved By: Alex Nguyen, Managing Director

1. Purpose and Scope

This policy establishes the framework for remote work arrangements and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) usage at NexusPoint Systems. Given our role as a managed IT services provider handling sensitive client infrastructure and data, this policy ensures security standards are maintained while supporting flexible work arrangements that enable our team to deliver exceptional service to our clients across Western Australia.

This policy applies to all NexusPoint Systems employees, contractors, and temporary staff who work remotely or use personal devices for business purposes.

2. Remote Work Eligibility and Approval

2.1 Eligible Positions

Remote work arrangements are available for roles that can be performed effectively outside the office environment. Generally, this includes:

  • Service delivery consultants
  • Cybersecurity analysts
  • Solutions architects
  • Administrative and finance roles
  • Project management positions

Service desk roles require prior approval from Sam Okoro due to specific equipment and monitoring requirements. Field service positions are assessed on a case-by-case basis by Priya Sharma.

2.2 Approval Process

  1. Complete Remote Work Application Form (available on SharePoint)
  2. Submit to direct supervisor for initial review
  3. Forward to Jordan Reeves for HR assessment
  4. Final approval by department head
  5. IT security assessment by Marcus Webb's team
  6. Completion of mandatory cybersecurity training module

Approval is typically granted for 6-12 month periods with regular reviews. Emergency remote work arrangements (e.g., family emergencies, public health situations) can be approved verbally by supervisors with paperwork completed within 5 business days.

2.3 Home Office Requirements

Remote workers must maintain a dedicated workspace that:

  • Has reliable broadband internet (minimum 25 Mbps down/5 Mbps up)
  • Includes proper ergonomic setup to prevent workplace injuries
  • Ensures privacy for confidential client calls and video conferences
  • Has backup power arrangements for critical work periods
  • Meets basic occupational health and safety standards

Note: The company provides a $500 one-time home office setup allowance for approved remote workers. Contact Tanya Brooks for reimbursement procedures.

3. BYOD Program Guidelines

3.1 Device Requirements

Personal devices used for business purposes must meet the following minimum specifications:

Mobile Devices: - iOS 15.0 or later / Android 12 or later - Device encryption enabled - Automatic screen lock (maximum 10 minutes) - Current operating system patches installed

Laptops/Computers: - Windows 11 Pro or macOS 12.0 or later - 8GB RAM minimum (16GB preferred) - SSD storage with full disk encryption - Current antivirus software with real-time protection - Automatic OS updates enabled

Note: Linux devices require specific approval from Liam Foster due to compatibility considerations with our client management tools.

3.2 Prohibited Devices

The following devices are not permitted for business use: - Jailbroken or rooted devices - Devices with known security vulnerabilities - Shared family devices - Devices older than 4 years (assessed case-by-case) - Gaming-focused devices without business-grade security features

3.3 Device Registration and Management

All BYOD devices must be:

  1. Registered through our Microsoft Intune portal
  2. Enrolled in Mobile Device Management (MDM)
  3. Configured with company security profiles
  4. Subjected to periodic compliance scans

Contact the Service Desk team for device enrollment assistance. The process typically takes 45-60 minutes and includes installation of required business applications.

4. Security Requirements

4.1 Network Security

VPN Usage: - All remote connections to company resources must use the FortiGate VPN client - VPN must be active for all business activities, including email access - Split tunneling is prohibited for security-sensitive roles - Personal internet usage should occur outside VPN connection when possible

Wi-Fi Security: - Avoid public Wi-Fi networks for business activities - Use WPA3 encryption on home networks (WPA2 minimum acceptable) - Guest networks must not be used for business purposes - Mobile hotspots are acceptable for temporary use with VPN active

4.2 Data Protection

Data Classification: - Client Confidential: Must remain on company-managed systems only - Internal Use: Can be accessed on BYOD devices with proper controls - Public: No restrictions on device usage

Storage and Backup: - Business data must not be stored locally on personal devices - Use OneDrive for Business or designated network shares only - Automatic backup of business data to personal cloud services is prohibited - USB drives for business data require encryption (contact IT for approved solutions)

4.3 Application Management

Required Applications: - Microsoft 365 suite (Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint) - ConnectWise Manage (for client-facing roles) - FortiGate VPN client - Microsoft Authenticator for multi-factor authentication

Prohibited Applications: - Unauthorized cloud storage services (Dropbox, Google Drive personal accounts) - Remote access tools not approved by IT (TeamViewer, AnyDesk) - Cryptocurrency mining software - P2P file sharing applications

Current approved application list is maintained by Marcus Webb and available on the IT Services SharePoint site.

5. Communication and Collaboration Standards

5.1 Availability and Responsiveness

Remote workers are expected to: - Maintain core business hours availability (9:00 AM - 3:00 PM AWST minimum) - Respond to urgent communications within 2 hours during business hours - Participate in scheduled team meetings via video conference - Update calendar with accurate availability information - Be reachable by phone during designated work hours

5.2 Video Conferencing Guidelines

  • Video should be enabled for internal team meetings
  • Professional background or background blur required for client meetings
  • Mute microphone when not speaking in large meetings
  • Test audio/video setup before important client presentations
  • Have backup audio option (phone dial-in) for critical meetings

5.3 Client Interaction Protocols

When working remotely during client interactions: - Ensure professional environment free from distractions - Use company email signature including full contact details - Maintain confidentiality of client information in shared spaces - Schedule client site visits from office location when possible for equipment access

6. Performance and Monitoring

6.1 Productivity Expectations

Remote work arrangements are contingent on maintaining performance standards equivalent to or exceeding office-based work. Key metrics include: - Client satisfaction scores (target: 4.5/5.0 or higher) - Response time to support tickets (per role-specific SLAs) - Project delivery timelines - Team collaboration and communication effectiveness

6.2 Monitoring and Compliance

The company reserves the right to: - Monitor network access logs for security compliance - Perform remote security scans of registered devices - Require return to office for performance or security concerns - Access business data on personal devices during investigations

Device monitoring is limited to business applications and data. Personal use monitoring does not occur, but clear separation of business and personal activities is expected.

7. Equipment and Reimbursements

7.1 Company-Provided Equipment

Standard remote work equipment package includes: - Laptop docking station or monitor (upon request) - Noise-cancelling headset for client calls - Ergonomic keyboard and mouse (if requested) - Mobile phone subsidy ($60/month for client-facing roles)

7.2 Internet and Utility Allowances

  • Internet reimbursement: $50/month for primary remote workers
  • Phone allowance: $40/month for roles requiring significant client communication
  • Utility allowance: Under review pending policy update (expected January 2025)

Contact Tanya Brooks for allowance setup and monthly reimbursement processing.

8. Data Breach and Incident Response

In the event of a suspected security incident:

  1. Immediate Actions:
  2. Disconnect affected device from network
  3. Do not shut down device (preserve evidence)
  4. Contact Marcus Webb immediately (mobile: 0418 555 0234)

  5. Document what occurred and when

  6. Reporting Requirements:

  7. Security incidents must be reported within 1 hour of discovery
  8. Complete Incident Report Form within 24 hours

  9. Cooperate fully with investigation and remediation efforts

  10. Device Isolation:

  11. Company reserves right to remotely wipe business data from compromised devices
  12. Personal data will be preserved where possible during remediation
  13. Replacement devices can be expedited for critical roles

9. Policy Compliance and Violations

9.1 Compliance Monitoring

Compliance with this policy is monitored through: - Monthly device compliance reports from MDM system - Quarterly security awareness training completion - Annual policy acknowledgment requirement - Incident investigation findings

9.2 Violation Consequences

Policy violations may result in: - Verbal or written warning - Mandatory additional cybersecurity training - Restriction or revocation of remote work privileges - Disciplinary action up to and including termination

Serious security violations (intentional data exposure, circumventing security controls) will result in immediate investigation and may lead to termination and legal action.

10. Support and Resources

10.1 Technical Support

  • Service Desk: servicedesk@nexuspoint.com.au or (08) 6555 4200
  • After-hours emergency: Contact on-call engineer via ConnectWise
  • VPN/connectivity issues: Priority support during business hours
  • Device enrollment assistance: Available Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

10.2 Policy Questions

  • General remote work questions: Jordan Reeves (jordan.reeves@nexuspoint.com.au)
  • Security-related queries: Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@nexuspoint.com.au)
  • Equipment and allowances: Tanya Brooks (tanya.brooks@nexuspoint.com.au)

Document History:

  • v1.0 (March 2021): Initial policy creation
  • v2.0 (August 2022): Major revision for post-COVID hybrid work model
  • v2.1 (December 2022): Updated VPN requirements following security audit
  • v2.2 (June 2023): Added mobile device specifications and BYOD guidelines
  • v2.3 (March 2024): Enhanced data classification requirements for cybersecurity expansion

Next Review Items: - Utility allowance implementation (pending budget approval) - Linux device support procedures - Integration with new security monitoring tools (Q4 2024)

This policy is subject to change based on business requirements, security threats, and regulatory updates. All employees will be notified of significant changes via email and required to acknowledge updated versions.
